summarization.a: Heat map showing the partial pressure of HF underneath the injector head of a spatial ALD tool, with a high pressure underneath the corresponding precursor slit.
summarization.b: Heat map showing the partial pressure of water underneath the injector head of a spatial ALD tool, the pressure is non-zero slightly above the surface of the substrate as water is the reaction product.

b.question: Why is there a hotspot of water pressure at the substrate?
b.answer: b	Water is the reaction product of HF with the substrate, so there is only water at the substrate and some in the purge lines.

b.question: Does the reaction of TMA with the substrate result in water as the reaction product?

a.question: Why is there HF in the slit right of the HF slit?
a.answer: a	This is HF that has not reacted at surface and is purged away.

a.question: Would this process be considered efficient in precursor usage?
a.answer: a	There is never 100% precursor efficiency, but it seems that there is a lot of HF pumped away.
